body:
## Summary

- Decouple GitHub Pages documentation deployment from package/GitHub release publication.
- Gate `deploy-docs` on successful `build` for main pushes and manual instant releases, so docs can still publish when package release fails later.
- Fix the observed `rust-script` release failure under `RUSTFLAGS=-Dwarnings`.
- Add an issue-3 case study with preserved CI run metadata, compressed failed-run logs, template workflow snapshots, and upstream-template follow-up.

Fixes #3

## Reproduction

The preserved main-branch runs `24979900862` and `24983875003` both showed this sequence:

1. `Build Package` succeeded.
2. `Auto Release` failed in `Check if version already released or no fragments`.
3. `Deploy Rust Documentation` was skipped because it depended on release jobs.

The detailed case study and raw data are in `docs/case-studies/issue-3/`.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo test --all-features --verbose`
- `cargo test --doc --verbose`
- `cargo test --test unit ci_cd::workflow_release::documentation_deploy_is_independent_from_release_publication`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`
- `RUSTFLAGS=-Dwarnings HAS_FRAGMENTS=true rust-script scripts/check-release-needed.rs`
- `cargo build --release --verbose`
- `cargo package --list --allow-dirty`

## Template Follow-Up

The Rust CI/CD template has the same `deploy-docs` dependency issue. I filed:

https://github.com/link-foundation/rust-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template/issues/38

comments.body:
## Solution summary

Implemented and pushed the fix to PR 4: https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-ontology/pull/4

What changed:
- `deploy-docs` now depends only on `build`, so GitHub Pages docs are no longer blocked by package/GitHub release jobs.
- Fixed the release helper scripts that were tripping `RUSTFLAGS=-Dwarnings`.
- Added a regression test for docs deployment independence.
- Added the requested case study/log/template evidence under `docs/case-studies/issue-3/`.
- Filed upstream template issue: https://github.com/link-foundation/rust-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template/issues/38
- Follow-up fixed the Windows CI failure by making the workflow test line-ending independent.

Verification:
- Local: `cargo fmt --check`
- Local: targeted workflow release test
- Local: `cargo test --all-features --verbose`
- Local: `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`
- GitHub Actions: fresh CI run `24985181894` passed on SHA `afa8dab3ecbd272506681b510ed545c302d24291`

PR is ready, not draft, merge state is clean, and the working tree is clean.
